user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.
claim_status_justification: The image does not provide clear and unambiguous evidence of the damage claim. The rear of the car appears to be dented, but without a comparison image or additional context, it is not possible to accurately assess the extent and severity of the damage.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.
claim_status_justification: Unable to evaluate.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images clearly show the front windshield of a vehicle with a crack emanating from an impact point. The damage is visible and identifiable.
claim_status_justification: The images provide clear visual evidence of the damage to the vehicle's windshield after a small stone impact. The extent of the crack is visible and indicates that this is not a minor surface scratch, but rather a significant impact with potential for further damage.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image is clear enough to assess the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image does not clearly show the claimed object or part, making it difficult to evaluate the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provide a clear view of the rear bumper and fender of the vehicle, allowing for inspection of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The images provided do not show the extent or location of the damage to the rear bumper. The claim requires additional context or a clearer view of the bumper for further evaluation.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.
claim_status_justification: The image provided shows a car on the road during sunset. The car appears to be in good condition, and there are no visible damages or scratches. To make an assessment, I would need a clearer image of the headlight in question. Please provide additional images from different angles that specifically show the headlight to accurately evaluate the claim.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a clear view of the laptop and the cracked screen, which is relevant to the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The image only shows the screen with a crack, and there is no visible damage to other parts of the laptop. To make a more accurate assessment, additional images showing the entire device or more context are required.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provide clear views of the laptop screen, keyboard, and side with ample context to evaluate the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The images do not clearly show the screen wobbling or the hinge area broken.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.
claim_status_justification: In the image provided, we can see water stains on the keyboard of a laptop. However, to assess the severity and validity of the claim, additional information is required. This includes the extent of the damage, the presence or absence of any internal damage not visible in the image, and confirmation that the user has followed proper care for electronics after the incident.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Unable to determine.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop with a visible dent on one corner. However, to determine if the damage is cosmetic or functional, and to properly assess the impact of this incident, additional context or images are required. The current image does not provide enough information to make an informed decision.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the laptop with visible screen damage, including cracks and shattered areas that are typical of glass shatter damage.
claim_status_justification: The user history does not provide a clear context for the damage. The images show that the screen is damaged, and the customer reported the issue correctly. However, without further information about the travel situation or any additional details on how the laptop was being carried, it is difficult to fully assess the claim. It could be a simple glass shatter or a more severe internal damage not visible in the image.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
